messages.content: Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What is the minimum delay required to claim compensation from Southeastern?
You can claim Delay Repay compensation if your train is delayed by 15 minutes or more, meaning you arrive at your destination at least 15 minutes late.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How long do I have to make a Delay Repay claim after my journey?
You have up to 28 days from the date of your delayed journey to submit a Delay Repay claim.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What are the three ways to claim Delay Repay with Southeastern?

The three ways are: 1) One Click Delay Repay for Key smartcard, eTicket, or Advance ticket customers who bought through the Southeastern website or app; 2) Online via the Delay Repay form; 3) By post using a downloaded claim form.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How does One Click Delay Repay work and who is eligible?

One Click Delay Repay automatically creates a claim for you when your journey is delayed. You just confirm the details. It's available to Key smartcard holders and anyone who buys an eTicket or Advance ticket through the Southeastern website or app for travel on the Southeastern network. You need to sign up by logging into your account and navigating to 'One Click Delay Repay'.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What payment methods can I choose for Delay Repay compensation?

You can receive compensation via bank transfer, PayPal, eVoucher (redeemable online), cashable voucher (exchangeable at ticket offices), or donate to one of five charities: Great Ormond Street Hospital Children's Charity, Cancer Research UK, Samaritans, RSPCA, or Railway Children.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How is Delay Repay compensation calculated for Oyster or Contactless payments?
Oyster and Contactless Pay As You Go are treated as single tickets for the journey taken. Compensation is based on the amount charged for that journey according to your tap-in and tap-out times.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What information do I need to provide when making a Delay Repay claim?

You need: date of travel, time of scheduled departure, origin station, any interchange stations, destination station. Also, depending on ticket type: a copy of your ticket (paper, Key card number, eTicket reference, etc.) or for Oyster/Contactless, a journey statement from TfL.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How do I claim Delay Repay if I used split tickets?

Enter your full journey details (origin and destination) without listing intermediate stations unless you made a connection. Use ticket number 00000, select 'Other' as the delay reason and type 'split ticket used' in the details box. Attach all tickets together in a single photo.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I use a receipt or confirmation email to claim Delay Repay?
No, you must provide a copy of the actual ticket because each ticket has a unique number that verifies it hasn't been claimed before. Receipts and confirmation emails do not include this number.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What if my journey involved multiple train operators and I was delayed?

You need to claim through the train operator that caused the first delay to the whole journey. If you submit a claim to Southeastern for a delay caused by another operator, they will pass it on and notify you.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I claim Delay Repay for a group of friends or family on one form?

No, you must make a separate claim for each person using their own name and contact details. Family members may share contact and payment details, but each person's name must be different on each claim.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What should I do if I activated a Flexi Season pass but then didn't travel due to disruption?

If you chose not to travel after activating because of disruption, contact Customer Services to have the pass added back. If there was no disruption, it cannot be reapplied. If you activated and then travelled later, you can claim Delay Repay for the earlier journey.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
I abandoned my journey due to delays and used another method to reach my destination. Can I claim?

Yes, you can claim Delay Repay for the delayed journey if you completed it by other means. If you returned to your origin or didn't travel at all (and you don't have a Season ticket), you should claim a refund instead.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Will Southeastern reimburse my taxi fare if I took one due to a delay?

Taxi claims are considered on a case-by-case basis under exceptional circumstances. You need to provide proof of your rail ticket and taxi receipt. During disruption, alternative transport like rail replacement buses may have been arranged, so taxi claims are reviewed individually.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
I missed my flight because of train delays. Can I get compensation?

You can contact Customer Services with your train ticket, flight booking, itinerary, and any receipts. They will review your case on a case-by-case basis. Note that no airports are directly on the Southeastern network, so allow plenty of time when travelling to the airport.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How long does it take to receive Delay Repay payment?

Bank transfers are usually paid the next working day after approval, but allow up to 48 hours. PayPal payments are typically within an hour, but allow up to 48 hours. eVouchers and cashable vouchers are issued after approval. Charity donations are sent the next working day.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What happens if my Delay Repay payment is rejected or I haven't received it?

Allow 14 working days for the transfer. If a faster payment or PayPal is rejected, the bank or PayPal notifies Southeastern (which can take up to 28 days), and then a National Rail Travel Voucher is automatically sent to you, usually within 4 weeks.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
My Delay Repay claim was declined. What can I do?
If you believe it was incorrectly assessed, reply to the email you received, or use the Contact Us form or call 0345 322 7021. Have your claim reference number ready.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What is Enhanced Compensation?

Enhanced Compensation provides additional compensation for sTicket, paper, and Key card Season ticket holders when a significant proportion of their journeys in a business period are delayed longer than 30 minutes. It is in addition to the standard Delay Repay.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How do I get a refund for a Daily Ticket bought on my Key card online but not used?

If today's date is before the travel date, you can process the refund online via your account. If it's the day of travel or up to 28 days after expiry and you haven't printed it, you need to fill out a refund form (a £5 admin fee applies). If it's more than 28 days after expiry, no refund is available.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What are the conditions for refunding a Weekly Season Ticket bought on a Key card online?
You can process a refund online if you have 3 days or more left on the ticket. A £5 admin fee applies, and the refund will be made via the original payment method.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How do I refund a Monthly Season Ticket on my Key card bought at a ticket office?
You need to go back to the local Southeastern station where you bought it, provided you have 7 days or more left on the ticket. A £5 admin fee applies. Check station opening times online.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I get a refund on a Flexi Season ticket?

Yes, a refund is calculated as the difference between the price paid and the cost of Anytime return tickets for each day used. You must apply before the 28-day validity ends, with at least one unused day pass left, and a £5 admin fee applies.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What if I was sick and couldn't use my Flexi Season ticket?

You can apply for a refund before the 28-day validity ends, with at least one unused day pass. If you provide evidence of illness (e.g., a doctor's note), Southeastern may consider applications after the expiry date.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
I bought smart tickets for others. How do I request a refund?

Only the purchaser can request a refund. Log into your online account, go to 'Manage my tickets / refund', select the tickets, and follow instructions. If bought with a group discount and you're no longer travelling together, the ticket becomes invalid.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What should I do if I chose the wrong Key card to send a ticket to?
Submit a refund request through your online account for that ticket, then repurchase it for the correct Key card. If you have concerns, contact Customer Services.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I get a refund on an eTicket if I decide not to travel?

Yes, the same terms apply as for paper tickets. The easiest way is to request a refund through your Southeastern account. If the eTicket has been scanned (used), you cannot refund online; contact Customer Services.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Are Advance tickets refundable?

Advance tickets are generally non-refundable. However, fee-free refunds may be available if the service is cancelled due to industrial action or if you abandon your journey due to disruption. For Advance tickets bought through Southeastern, contact Customer Services; otherwise, contact your original retailer.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I change the date or time of an Advance ticket?

Yes, Advance Singles can be amended with a £10 admin fee per ticket. You can change the date, time, or route as long as it's with the same train operator. Origin and destination cannot be changed. Amendments can be made online up to the day before travel, or at a station up to departure time.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I reserve a specific seat on Southeastern trains?
No, it is not possible to reserve a specific seat. With an Advance ticket, you can sit anywhere on the train you are booked on.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What is GroupSave and who qualifies?

GroupSave offers a 1/3 discount on adult tickets for groups of 3 to 9 people travelling together. It is valid on Super Off-Peak, Off-Peak, and some Anytime tickets during off-peak times (after 09:30 weekdays, all day weekends and bank holidays). All tickets must be bought together in one transaction.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I combine GroupSave with Railcard discounts?
No, GroupSave cannot be used with Railcards or any other offers. It is a separate discount.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What is PlusBus and how does it work?

PlusBus is a discount bus pass that you buy with your train ticket. It gives unlimited bus travel on participating operators' services within the town's travel zone. You can buy it at booking offices, ticket machines, or online. Prices start from £2 a day, and children pay half. Railcard holders get 1/3 off.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Which stations on the Southeastern network offer PlusBus?

PlusBus is available at many stations including Ashford International, Broadstairs, Canterbury East, Canterbury West, Chatham, Dartford, Dover Priory, Ebbsfleet International, Folkestone Central, Hastings, Maidstone East, Margate, Ramsgate, Tonbridge, Tunbridge Wells, and more. See the full list on the Southeastern website.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Do I need to buy a ticket before boarding a Southeastern train?

Yes, according to the National Rail Conditions of Travel, you must buy a ticket before boarding. If you board without a ticket and ticket-buying options were available at the station, you may be liable for a penalty fare. Only undiscounted Anytime fares are available from the conductor.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What is the Ticket Vending Machine Price Guarantee?

If you buy a ticket from a Southeastern ticket vending machine and find the same ticket available elsewhere for a lower price, you can contact Customer Services with copies of the tickets to claim a refund of the difference.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I use Oyster or Contactless on Southeastern trains?

Yes, you can use Oyster or Contactless (including Apple Pay and Google Pay) on most National Rail services in London, including some Southeastern routes within the London Travelcard zones. Remember to touch in and out.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What facilities are available for passengers travelling with children?

Southeastern provides information on baby changing facilities, step-free access, and a SeatFinder tool to find less busy trains. You can request a 'baby on board' badge or priority seating card. Onboard, there are baby changing toilets in the accessible coach. Wi-Fi is available and filtered for safe browsing.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How should I store a pram or buggy on the train?

You can use the wheelchair-accessible area if unoccupied, but must give up the space if a wheelchair user needs it. Prams must be foldable. It's recommended to bring a sling or carrier for easier travel.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Are there baby changing facilities on Southeastern trains?
Yes, baby changing toilets are located in the accessible coach, usually marked by an orange strip above the door and a wheelchair symbol.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What are the rules for taking a bike on Southeastern trains?

Folding bikes (wheels 20 inches or smaller) are allowed anytime if folded. Regular bikes are allowed off-peak (anytime weekends and bank holidays) and on some peak services except on certain routes. Bikes are free, first come first served. Wheelchair spaces have priority. E-bikes (road-legal) are allowed under same rules, but charging is not permitted.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Are e-scooters allowed on Southeastern trains?
No, e-scooters and e-skateboards are banned due to fire safety concerns with lithium-ion batteries. This policy is enforced under the National Rail Conditions of Travel and Railway Byelaws.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I take a tandem or tricycle on a Southeastern train?

No, tandems, tricycles, handbikes, motorcycles, or cycles with trailers cannot be accommodated. If you use a cycle as a mobility aid and it is no larger than 1200mm long and 700mm wide, contact Southeastern to explore options.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What are Cycle Hubs and how do I access them?

Cycle Hubs are secure, covered bike parking facilities with lighting and CCTV at 29 stations. You can register for a 3, 6, or 12-month subscription at the station ticket office. Prices vary by tier (e.g., £15-£40 for 12 months). You'll receive a key fob for access.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How much does it cost to use a Cycle Hub?

Subscription prices depend on the station tier: Tier 1: £15 (3 months), £25 (6 months), £40 (12 months); Tier 2: £10, £20, £30; Tier 3: £5, £15, £20. See the list of stations in each tier on the Southeastern website.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What is an sTicket and how do I use it?

An sTicket is a digital Season ticket stored in the Southeastern app. You buy it online or via the app, upload a photo (for most seasons), and then access it in 'My Tickets'. Scan the barcode at gates or show to staff. The barcode refreshes automatically every three hours or can be manually refreshed with an internet connection.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What if I have no internet connection when using an sTicket?
The app provides a backup barcode that allows you to travel. To receive backup barcodes, you need to enable push notifications in the app.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I claim Delay Repay with an sTicket?
Yes, you can claim via One Click Delay Repay through the app or website if you sign up. It works similarly to the Key smartcard.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What are the different ticket types available for purchase?

Southeastern offers Advance, Off-Peak, Super Off-Peak, Anytime, Season, Flexi Season, Contactless, and Oyster tickets. Each has different validity, time restrictions, and refund policies. See the fare table on their website for details.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
How do I get a refund for a paper Daily ticket bought online and already printed?

You need to send the printed ticket(s) to Southeastern Customer Services at PO Box 8625, Swadlincote, DE11 1HZ. If the refund is requested before the travel date, no admin fee; if on or after, a £5 admin fee applies. Advance tickets are non-refundable.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Can I change my Off-Peak or Super Off-Peak ticket?
No, these tickets are non-amendable. You would need to apply for a refund (within 28 days of travel) and purchase a new ticket. Admin fees may apply.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
What is the deadline to buy Advance tickets?

As of 27 February 2026, weekday Advance tickets are available until Friday 5 June 2026, and weekend bookings until Sunday 31 May 2026. Services departing between 23:00 and 02:00 open 12 weeks in advance. Check for updates.
Reply user's question as accurately as possible.
Where can I buy Advance tickets?

Advance tickets can be purchased online, through the app, or at station ticket offices. They are not available at Ticket Vending Machines. They come as paper or eTickets, but not on the Key smartcard.
